title: The Breach
narrative: Dolohov is down. But the castle shudders — a section of the outer wall has given way. Through the dust comes a new wave of Death Eaters flooding the Entrance Hall below. McGonagall's voice carries over the chaos: three corridors need to be sealed — this one, the staircase above, and the passage to the library. Remus and Tonks cannot hold all three. The corridor they stand in is the widest — the most dangerous if left open. They have enough magic between them to seal one corridor with Protego Maxima and Reparo, or to fight the advancing wave head-on.

options.text: Seal this corridor now with Protego Maxima and fall back to regroup with the Order.
options.feedback_hint: The barrier holds long enough for the Order to plug the other two passages. Remus and Tonks reach safety intact.

options.text: Hold the corridor by force — fight each wave until reinforcements arrive.
options.feedback_hint: The waves don't stop. Fighting without a barrier costs too much — they're surrounded before the third wave breaks.

options.text: Split the work — Remus seals this corridor, Tonks takes the library passage alone.
options.feedback_hint: Splitting up leaves Tonks alone against whatever waits in the library passage. The same mistake, different corridor.

free_text.prompt: How do you secure the corridor?

categories.label: Seal the corridor with a barrier spell and retreat or regroup together
Fight the waves directly, hold position, stand and duel the oncoming Death Eaters
Separate to cover multiple corridors, send Tonks alone to another passage

free_text.llm_classification_criteria: Classify into exactly one category. SEAL_AND_FALL_BACK when the player uses a barrier or sealing spell and then retreats or regroups with allies. HOLD_BY_FORCE when the player stays to fight without a sealing strategy. SPLIT_UP when the player separates Remus and Tonks to cover different corridors. INVALID for off-topic or nonsense.
